front: Novel: Ivan tells Alyosha the parable of the Grand Inquisitor, in which Christ returns to 16th-century Seville and is imprisoned by a church leader who argues the church follows the Devil's principles
Novel: Ivan declares "if there is no immortality, then everything is permitted" and tells his brother "I most respectfully return him the ticket"
Novel: Dmitri is tried for patricide though Smerdyakov is the real killer; evidence includes 3,000 rubles and a brass pestle; Grushenka tells the parable of the onion
Novel: Raskolnikov murders a pawnbroker with an axe; inspector Porfiry questions him about his article arguing "extraordinary" people can "transgress the law"
Novel: Raskolnikov forces the prostitute Sonia to read the story of Lazarus; she later follows him to Siberian exile
Novel: the murder is foreshadowed by a dream of Mikolka beating a mare to death and a student calling the pawnbroker a parasite
Novel: Svidrigailov shoots himself with Dunya's revolver after telling a soldier he's headed for "America"
Novella: opens "I am a sick man... I am a spiteful man"; the narrator ridicules Chernyshevsky's Crystal Palace and the prostitute Liza in "Apropos of the Wet Snow"
Novel: the epileptic Prince Myshkin views Holbein's Dead Christ; Nastasya Filippovna throws 100,000 rubles into a fireplace; Rogozhin murders Nastasya
Novel: an allegory about nihilists led by Stavrogin; the censored chapter "At Tikhon's" describes a confession of rape; Kirillov kills himself to prove conquest of fear
Novel: a fictionalized Siberian prison memoir featuring a Dantesque scene where steam turns red off men taking a Christmas bath
back: The House of the Dead (Fyodor Dostoevsky)

front: Short Story: a lonely narrator wanders St. Petersburg and develops feelings for Nastenka, who is dismayed when her actual lover returns
Author: imprisoned after a mock execution for the Fourierist Petrashevsky Circle; spent four years in a Siberian prison camp
Author: Bakhtin's Problems of Dostoevsky's Poetics introduced "polyphony" and "unfinalizability" through studying this author's novels
Author: suffered from epilepsy (Geschwind syndrome); the "white bear problem" comes from this author's travel notes
Author: Nabokov parodied this author as Hermann Karlovich in Despair; J.M. Coetzee fictionalized him in The Master of Petersburg
